text.source_text: किं ताव त्प्राप्तम् ? बुद्धिक्षेत्रज्ञाविति । कुतः ? ‘ गुहां प्रविष्टौ ’ इति विशेषणात् । यदि शरीरं गुहा , यदि वा हृदयम् , उभयथापि बुद्धिक्षेत्रज्ञौ गुहां प्रविष्टावुपपद्येते । न च सति सम्भवे सर्वगतस्य ब्रह्मणो विशिष्टदेशत्वं युक्तं कल्पयितुम् । ‘ सुकृतस्य लोके ’ इति च कर्मगोचरानतिक्रमं दर्शयति । परमात्मा तु न सुकृतस्य वा दुष्कृतस्य वा गोचरे वर्तते , ‘ न कर्मणा वर्धते नो कनीयान् ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ४ । २३) इति श्रुतेः । ‘ छायातपौ ’ इति च चेतनाचेतनयोर्निर्देश उपपद्यते , छायातपवत्परस्परविलक्षणत्वात् । तस्माद्बुद्धिक्षेत्रज्ञाविहोच्येयातामित्येवं प्राप्ते ब्रूमः —
text.transliteration_iast: kiṃ tāva tprāptam ? buddhikṣetrajñāviti | kutaḥ ? ‘ guhāṃ praviṣṭau ’ iti viśeṣaṇāt | yadi śarīraṃ guhā , yadi vā hṛdayam , ubhayathāpi buddhikṣetrajñau guhāṃ praviṣṭāvupapadyete | na ca sati sambhave sarvagatasya brahmaṇo viśiṣṭadeśatvaṃ yuktaṃ kalpayitum | ‘ sukṛtasya loke ’ iti ca karmagocarānatikramaṃ darśayati | paramātmā tu na sukṛtasya vā duṣkṛtasya vā gocare vartate , ‘ na karmaṇā vardhate no kanīyān ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 4 | 23) iti śruteḥ | ‘ chāyātapau ’ iti ca cetanācetanayornirdeśa upapadyate , chāyātapavatparasparavilakṣaṇatvāt | tasmādbuddhikṣetrajñāvihocyeyātāmityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —

translation.literal_translation: What is prima facie obtained is: intellect and knower-self. Why? Because of the qualifier 'entered the cave.' Whether cave means body or heart, intellect and knower-self can coherently be said to have entered there. And where that is possible, one should not force a spatially particular location upon all-pervasive Brahman. The phrase 'in the world of merit' also indicates not crossing karma-domain; but the Supreme does not move within merit/demerit range ('neither increased by good nor diminished by bad'). Also 'shadow and light' fits a conscious-inert pair, due to mutual contrast. Therefore one may take the pair here as intellect and knower-self. To that we reply -
translation.technical_translation: Purvapaksha refinement in 1.2.11: guhapravesha, sukrita-loka limitation, and chaya-tapa contrast are marshaled for buddhi-jiva pairing.
translation.interpretive_note: Raises the strongest internal-semantic case against paramatma as one of the two.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Purvapaksa proposal deriving dual referents from guha-locus plausibility.
Reasoning device that makes interior cognitive pairings appear textually natural.

lexical_notes.gloss: improbability claim against all-pervasive Brahman localization
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Objection that specific site-entry language should not apply to Brahman under ordinary reading.
Used to prefer finite experiential subjects in the prima facie construal.
Frames the initial, eventually rebutted pairing strategy.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit sattva-kshetrajna and bhoktrtva figurative-usage choices in records 1.2.025-1.2.030.
